component.name: Command
component.description: Searchable command palette for actions and navigation.

commonPatterns.name: command-dialog
commonPatterns.description: Full command palette opened via keyboard shortcut

commonPatterns.name: inline-command
commonPatterns.description: Embedded searchable list without a dialog

commonPatterns.name: grouped-commands
commonPatterns.description: Commands organized into labeled groups with separators

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Command as a simple dropdown select
antiPatterns.reason: Command is optimized for search/filter workflows; simple selects add unnecessary complexity
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Select for straightforward option picking

antiPatterns.reason: Users see a blank list when no results match, which feels broken
antiPatterns.alternative: Always include CommandEmpty with a helpful message

antiPatterns.scenario: Command items without keyboard shortcut hints in a command palette
antiPatterns.reason: Users expect to discover shortcuts in a command palette
antiPatterns.alternative: Add CommandShortcut to frequently used actions

slots.CommandInput: Search input with a search icon
slots.CommandEmpty: Message shown when no items match the filter
slots.CommandGroup: Labeled group of related command items
slots.CommandShortcut: Keyboard shortcut hint aligned to the right

interactions.typing: Filters visible items in real-time via cmdk

accessibility.keyboardSupport: Arrow keys to navigate, Enter to select, Escape to close, typing to filter
accessibility.screenReader: Items announce their label; groups announce headings; empty state announced
accessibility.focusManagement: Focus trapped in dialog mode; auto-focuses input on open

rules: Use CommandDialog for overlay command palettes, Command for inline use
Group related items with CommandGroup and use headings
CommandDialog wraps Dialog internally — do not nest inside another Dialog

aiHints.context: Use for searchable command menus and action palettes. Built on cmdk. CommandDialog provides a full modal experience; Command alone is for inline embedding. Always include CommandEmpty for no-results feedback.
